rights.basis: The repository artifact represents target passages from the London-published 1904–1905 editions. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the work would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the later 1905 edition expired no later than 2000. This U.S.-only row covers represented edition text, not the XML serialization or markup, and makes no claim about source-country status.

next_steps: Ask a TLA archivist for rebinding, conservation, accession, and old-to-new shelfmark documentation.
Request a complete old-folio-to-current-component concordance before adding same_as, contains, or direct transcribes relations.
openQuestions.question: What exact institutional event and folio concordance relate Littlehales’s single 821-leaf Guildhall MS 1239/1 state to current /001/001, /001/002, and /001/003?

next_steps: Ask TLA to reconcile the generic scope note, component date ranges, opening losses, and retained original foliation.
openQuestions.question: Why does the edition begin in 1420 while current /001/001 begins in 1422, and what does the repeated current catalog text ‘I, 1431–1553; w.&d.’ mean?

next_steps: Authenticate with a History Card or ask TLA staff to confirm production and condition status for each exact reference.
Record item-specific restrictions without generalizing one component’s status to the others.
openQuestions.question: Can each of the four exact current Document records be ordered with a History Card today, and are any surfaces closed, at risk, unfit, surrogate-only, or otherwise restricted?

next_steps: Submit a separate TLA copying assessment for each exact component.
Ask whether existing service or preservation images can be supplied before commissioning new capture.
Require explicit all-surfaces coverage and separate private-study and publication terms.
openQuestions.question: Do public, preservation, or service scans exist for each component, or will TLA assess complete all-surfaces reproduction requests with coverage, condition, price, format, and use terms?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for a component-level and jurisdiction-specific interpretation.
Retain both dated rights layers until U-M answers explicitly.
openQuestions.question: Does the current public-domain/No Copyright-US item statement supersede the embedded 2003 redistribution restriction, and what exact digital components and jurisdictions does each statement cover?

next_steps: Obtain an explicit project statement before assigning the Unlicense to the XML/data/markup layer.
Keep represented public-domain edition text distinct from repository serialization rights.
openQuestions.question: Does CME’s root Unlicense, whose text calls its subject software, cover inherited XML, data, or markup?

next_steps: Ask Internet Archive whether direct-file availability is intended and whether item-specific rights or access terms exist.

reviewNotes: The graph has exactly 12 entities, 12 relations, 24 access records, 18 rights records, 30 evidence records, and 8 open questions.
Historical MS A remains separate from current /001/001–003; no same_as, contains, or direct transcribes edge is asserted without an institutional concordance.
MS B contributes exactly one scoped memorandum: XML ajt8135:4.6.3, printed p. 240, account year 1500–01, historical leaf 62.
The two Littlehales editions and two Internet Archive carrier entities remain distinct; no merged 1904–1905 carrier is invented.
All 11 local copies occur exactly once across three access records and total 27,788,881 bytes: four target-present complete PDF/OCR objects and seven target-absent metadata-only objects.

All 11 worktree cache files are byte-identical hardlinks to the canonical main cache, with matching inode, link count 2, and verified SHA-256; cached IA size and MD5 values also matched live file metadata on 2026-07-16.

Every modeled entity has at least one rights row and every rights row carries an access_id. Current U-M NoC-US, retained 2003 provider terms, edition public-domain status, repository markup uncertainty, IA scan uncertainty, and TLA reproduction terms remain separate layers.
No relation says the repository artifact consulted research sources merely because those sources were used to build this manifest.

sourceLinks.accessMethod: Read the exact pinned CME submodule path
sourceLinks.entityLabel: General Corpus source XML for ajt8135

rights.basis: The repository artifact represents target passages from the London-published 1904–1905 editions. Under 17 U.S.C. § 104A(a)(1)(B), any restored copyright could subsist only for the remainder of the U.S. term the work would otherwise have received; § 304 caps the relevant pre-1978 term at 95 years from securing. Even assuming maximum U.S. protection, the later 1905 edition expired no later than 2000. This U.S.-only row covers represented edition text, not the XML serialization or markup, and makes no claim about source-country status.
rights.component: Represented target text from the 1904–1905 Littlehales edition

rights.basis: CME’s root LICENSE contains the Unlicense text but explicitly calls its subject software. No evidence establishes that it grants rights in inherited XML, data, or markup, so no license is assigned to this layer.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions

lineage.summary: The pinned repository XML copies U-M CME item ajt8135, which encodes a bounded V–VI selection from two distinct Littlehales EETS carriers: 1904 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 61–356, and 1905 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 357–413. Historical MS A supplies the continuous text; MS B contributes only the memorandum at p. 240, leaf 62. Historical MS A remains separate from the three current TLA /001 components because no institutional split or folio concordance was found. Eleven fixity-verified cache objects represent the two Internet Archive carriers and the official TLA catalog PDF.

catalogSummary.author: Parish church of St Mary-at-Hill and successive churchwardens and accounting clerks
catalogSummary.date: Represented records 1420–1559; source editions 1904 and 1905; U-M encoding 2003
catalogSummary.editor: Henry Littlehales (1904–1905); University of Michigan Library electronic encoding (2003)
catalogSummary.region: St Mary-at-Hill, City of London, England
catalogSummary.title: Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda of St Mary-at-Hill, 1420–1559 (Littlehales Chapters V–VI selection)

catalogingSubject.label: Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda of St Mary-at-Hill, 1420–1559 (Littlehales Chapters V–VI selection)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The exact bounded editorial unit encoded by ajt8135: the 1904 and 1905 edition title leaves [iii]–[iv] and Littlehales Chapters V–VI, printed pp. 61–413. It is not the whole two-part edition, one authored medieval work, either manuscript as a whole, or all surviving St Mary-at-Hill records. The inherited U-M source note says Chapters IV and V, but the body headings, table of contents, and continuous printed pagination identify Chapters V and VI.

contentParts.label: Part I title-page paratext
notes: Non-count-bearing editorial paratext; its four L elements do not change the prose form of the cataloged selection.
contentParts.summary: The encoded title leaves of the 1904 EETS OS 125 carrier, including bibliographic matter, a record quotation, and a four-line Longfellow epigraph.

contentParts.label: V. Churchwardens’ Accounts
notes: MS A is the continuous source stream. The one explicitly scoped MS B memorandum occurs later in Chapter VI, at p. 240.
contentParts.summary: Churchwardens’ accounts from 1420 through the mid-1490s, printed from MS A and including receipts, payments, church fabric, rents, chantries, burials, inventories, and parish administration.

contentParts.label: Part II title-page paratext
notes: The XML places this title page in FRONT before both body chapters; that serialization does not make it historically precede Chapter V.
contentParts.summary: The encoded title leaves of the distinct 1905 EETS OS 128 carrier, including bibliographic matter and a repeated four-line Longfellow epigraph.

contentParts.label: VI. Churchwardens’ Accounts and Memoranda

locators.value: ajt8135:4; PB 217–413; MS B memorandum at ajt8135:4.6.3, p. 240

notes: The chapter spans both printed carriers. The single MS B memorandum does not create a second whole-chapter transmission stream.
contentParts.summary: The continuation through 1559, selectively edited from MS A after 1495 while retaining the annual frame and distinct-value matter. It includes exactly one explicitly identified MS B contribution: the 1500–01 memorandum on p. 240 from MS B leaf 62, with a less explicit MS A parallel noted by Littlehales.

assertion.notes: This is the range of records represented by the bounded selection, not one composition event, binding date, or publication date.

extent.description: Two intellectual sections, Chapters V and VI. No defensible intellectual-item total is assigned: 82 DIV2 account-year containers, 563 DIV3 containers, 724 P elements, 578 tables, and 5,861 rows are encoding/editorial metrics, and 33 subordinate DIV3 containers are explicit omission placeholders rather than records. The four content parts are non-count-bearing. Eight L elements are repeated title-page epigraph lines and do not count as intrinsic verse.

formStatements.description: The accounts and memoranda are prose. Eight serialized L elements are two copies of a four-line Longfellow title-page epigraph and are editorial paratext, not intrinsic verse.

scope.description: Parish churchwardens’ accounts, memoranda, inventories, and related administrative record matter
Primary language of the earlier account material and still represented in the later selection
Early Modern English in the later sixteenth-century records

languageStatements.label: Latin
notes: Greek is excluded: it is declared only in the inherited header and no Greek target text or body-level language assignment was found.

scope.description: Recurrent administrative formulas, headings, and dating expressions

notes: The inherited U-M source note says Chapters IV and V; the encoded headings, contents, and pagination prove that the retained chapters are V and VI.

MS A is the sole continuous manuscript stream. MS B contributes only the explicitly scoped 1500–01 memorandum at p. 240, leaf 62; the less explicit MS A parallel is an editorial note, not a second transmitted text.
The historical one-volume MS A description and the three current TLA /001 components remain separate lineage evidence layers until an institutional concordance is obtained.
Witness holdings, component access, facsimile completeness, reproduction terms, and rights conflicts remain in the linked lineage manifest.
Associated place is used instead of inventing a single place of authorship for every entry.

subjects.label: The church as building, institution, and Christian community

scope.description: Burials, obits, chantries, and commemorative expenditure within the account selection

summaries.value: An edition-defined selection of late-medieval and early-modern parish churchwardens’ accounts and memoranda from St Mary-at-Hill in London. The records document receipts, expenditure, church fabric, rents, liturgical and commemorative activity, personnel, burials, inventories, and parish administration from 1420 to 1559. Littlehales printed MS A in full through 1494–95 and selected later matter while preserving an annual frame; one memorandum at p. 240 is explicitly supplied from MS B, leaf 62.

The electronic item joins bounded material from two distinct EETS carriers: 1904 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 61–356, followed by 1905 title leaves [iii]–[iv] and pp. 357–413. The XML serializes both title-page divisions before both body chapters. Printed pp. 1–60 and 414–449, unprinted manuscript matter, and all other P69/MRY4 records are outside the cataloging subject.

assertion.notes: The supplied qualifier makes the edition-defined V–VI selection boundary explicit.

notes: Exact inherited U-M 245 title; the manifest does not adopt the erroneous inherited Chapters IV–V source note.

work.summary: An edition-defined selection of late-medieval and early-modern parish churchwardens’ accounts and memoranda from St Mary-at-Hill in London. The records document receipts, expenditure, church fabric, rents, liturgical and commemorative activity, personnel, burials, inventories, and parish administration from 1420 to 1559. Littlehales printed MS A in full through 1494–95 and selected later matter while preserving an annual frame; one memorandum at p. 240 is explicitly supplied from MS B, leaf 62.
